## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's revisit the concept of the three audiences that we talked about early on in this program. So everything in the webinar sales funnel is based on one thing, whether or not you have their email address. Because if you have their email address, then you can put other promotional emails around your webinar. So you're not relying just on your webinar to actually make sales. So if you've got your their email address, then you can send them to a replay promotion. You can send them through a sales page promotion. There's lots of other things that you can do that if you don't have their email address, there's no way to be able to do that other than potentially something like a retargeting campaign, which just doesn't get the vast majority of people. And so this concept of the email address and getting it is really key in running a successful webinar sales funnel. Here's where the three audiences come in. Because when you run a webinar, you basically have these three potential audiences. The first one is internal traffic, where you have their email address, either they were on your list before you began promoting your webinar, or they you brought in, brought people into the webinar funnel in a way that you collect their email address before you give them the opportunity to choose whether they're coming to a webinar or not. Either way, you end up having their email address. Those people are gold because you can continue to promote throughout the webinar sales funnel to them. The second batch is the group I'm calling external traffic. These are people where you don't have their email address. A great example of this is when you're running ads directly to a webinar sign up page. And when they get there, the people who don't sign up for your webinar, you don't have an email address for them. So you don't have any other way to be able to communicate with them other than a retargeting ad, as I mentioned. The third group, which is part of external traffic, but not quite the same is affiliate traffic. This is the case where you don't have their email address, but your affiliate does. And so there's a strategy that I'm going to show you in a moment that you can use with your affiliates who promote you promote a webinar for you is to still get their email addresses of their people so that you can can take them through the rest of the webinar sales funnel. Now the strategy for converting each of these three audiences different, but at the same time, it's very much the same. You're going to see it looks the same after a certain point because strategically, the key difference in promoting to these three different audiences is comes in what happens prior to the webinar. But then for the 70% who won't sign up for the webinar, much less even a 10 one, it's

## [03:00 - 06:03]

after the webinar is held that we actually kick into high marketing mode. So the key question here is where in the webinar sales funnel that I just showed you in a previous video, where do we take that group of people who didn't sign up for the webinar and put them back into that funnel? The answer is at the point of the replay promo. So if you remember, I showed you this so you already have their email, you send it to the webinar registration, you promote to attending the webinar, then did they attend the webinar or not? If they didn't buy, then you promote the replay. So here's what we do with that for these other audiences. So if you take out the group of people who you already have their email address, and now we look at external traffic, the people who we don't have their email address. So external traffic, we send them first to a webinar registration page. And by the way, I actually recommend that, and we talked about this way back in one of the early modules that you develop. If you're running ads to webinar, the ideal method is to run ads into an opt-in incentive. And then so you get their email address and then you promote your webinar. But in cases where you don't do that, that's where this applies. Okay. So external traffic, you drive them to a webinar registration page. If they do register for the webinar, you now have their email address and you can go through the entire rest of the funnel. But if they don't, then you run a retargeting and advertising retargeting campaign, which sends them to an opt-in offer. Once they sign up for that, then we deliver that opt-in offer and probably a couple of other emails to nurture them. And then we bring them in to the replay promo. And so that's where they enter back into the funnel. So if they say no to the webinar registration, we can't get them to attend the webinar, but we can show them the replay from the webinar. All right. So that's how we handle this external traffic, the people who we do not have their email address. Now, let's look at the affiliate promo. So a place where you don't have their email address, but your affiliate does. And so here's the base campaign, the way it works already with your list. And then when we're working with an affiliate promoting it, what we do is we have the affiliate

## [06:03 - 07:32]

mail to a webinar registration page. If they register again, they go through the rest of the funnel. If they don't, then we have the email affiliate make a second email campaign to an opt-in form. So remember 70% of people are not going to sign up for the webinar. And so every time you talk to an affiliate about mailing to a webinar, you want to make it clear that there's actually going to be two separate campaigns that are going to mail to the first campaign is mail to my webinar. The second campaign, Mr. Affiliate is since 70% of people aren't going to sign up for that webinar, I also recommend that you mail to an opt-in campaign after the webinar registration. And then what we'll do with that is we'll then get them to opt into my emails and then I'll send them some emails that deliver what we promised them as well as nurture them. And then I'll bring them in at the point of the replay promo and they're now back in the webinar sales funnel. And so we can continue to work with them through all those other elements. And so that way you, Mr. Mrs. Affiliate, you make more money because not only do we get the people who are willing to sign up for a webinar, but we get that huge group of people who won't and give them an opportunity still to buy my product so I can send you great big checks.
